wiki.summary: On “nuts,” the two artists talk about their past love experiences on a fast-paced beat taken directly from the repertoire of Lil Peep’s friend and former collaborator Willie G. Gus departs from his usual grunge-sounding rap to bring a more sung touch that contrasts with the other tracks of LIVE FOREVER, considered to be one of Peep’s coldest, slowest, and darkest mixtape. The girl Peep refers to in his verse is his ex-girlfriend Emma Harris. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: On “nuts,” the two artists talk about their past love experiences on a fast-paced beat taken directly from the repertoire of Lil Peep’s friend and former collaborator Willie G. Gus departs from his usual grunge-sounding rap to bring a more sung touch that contrasts with the other tracks of LIVE FOREVER, considered to be one of Peep’s coldest, slowest, and darkest mixtape. The girl Peep refers to in his verse is his ex-girlfriend Emma Harris. She and the Long Island native met in elementary school and spent most of their schooling together before breaking up shortly after leaving high school. Although it is one of the last LIVE FOREVER tracks still available on Peep’s SoundCloud, “nuts” streams soared in summer 2020 after the song went viral on TikTok. Read more on Last.fm.
